item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem man, checking your draft was actually pretty refreshing — after having read some less… well-composed writings on that day.

Too bad most of the attention is poured into the 4000 Contest now, or this article would have got more upvotes. Shame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this; it subverted my expectations in a number of interesting ways. I'm pleased there wasn't some sort of final amazing twist (just having the final song cause migraines was an excellent/melancholy way to end it). One note:

Subject Was unresponsive to external stimuli for 28 minutes.

I suspect the 'Was' shouldn't have been capitalized, so I changed it to lower-case; if I'm in error, feel free to change it back.

I also agree with Shaggydredlocks above; this piece could probably be shorter, but that's a fairly mild criticism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Genuinely heartbreaking, though I think you could do without the second interview. I picked up on what was happening in the test log right away, so that bit of exposition felt unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My newest SCP. Big thanks to HumidHermit for providing critique.

Image was created by William Waterway and is borrowed from here.

To see more of my work, check out my Author Page!
